services.title: Panel Upgrade & Replacement
services.description: Upgrade your electrical panel to meet modern capacity demands and current code requirements.
services.intro: An outdated or undersized electrical panel is a safety hazard and a bottleneck for everything from EV chargers to home additions. We replace aging panels with modern, code-compliant units backed by a full workmanship warranty.
features: Full load calculation to right-size your new panel
Licensed permit pulled and inspected by local authority
200A or 400A service upgrades available
Replacement of Federal Pacific, Zinsco, or other recalled brands
New breakers, bus bars, and grounding electrode system
Arc-fault and ground-fault breakers installed per current NEC
Utility coordination for meter base upgrades
Final inspection walk-through and certificate of completion

process.title: Free Assessment
process.desc: We evaluate your current panel, load demand, and code deficiencies — no charge, no obligation.

process.title: Permit & Scheduling
process.desc: We pull the permit and coordinate with the utility so there are no surprises on installation day.

process.title: Same-Day Install
process.desc: Most panel upgrades are completed in a single day. We schedule the inspector so you're cleared before we leave.

process.title: Final Inspection
process.desc: We meet the inspector on-site, handle any punch-list items, and hand you a copy of your certificate.

faqs.q: How do I know if I need a panel upgrade?
faqs.a: Signs include frequently tripping breakers, flickering lights, inability to add new circuits, or a panel older than 25–30 years. Federal Pacific or Zinsco panels should be replaced regardless of age due to known safety defects.

faqs.q: Will I need to be home all day?
faqs.a: Power will be off for 4–8 hours during the upgrade. We try to schedule inspections for the same day so your power is restored before the inspector leaves.
Yes, always. A permit ensures the work is inspected and documented — protecting your homeowner's insurance and your home's resale value.

services.title: EV Charger Installation
services.description: Level 2 home EV charger installation with permit — charge overnight, drive every day.
services.intro: A Level 2 home charger adds 20–30 miles of range per hour — compared to 4–5 miles from a standard outlet. We handle the full installation including panel assessment, dedicated circuit, and utility rebate documentation.
features: Panel capacity assessment before any work begins
Dedicated 240V/50A circuit installed to code
NEMA 14-50 outlet or hardwired charger per your preference
All major charger brands supported (ChargePoint, Tesla, Wallbox, JuiceBox)
Permit pulled and inspected in every jurisdiction
Outdoor GFCI-rated installations available
Utility rebate documentation provided
Future-ready wiring for second charger if needed

process.title: Site Assessment
process.desc: We check your panel capacity, garage layout, and preferred charger location to plan the cleanest, safest install.

process.title: Equipment Selection
process.desc: We recommend the best charger for your vehicle and usage — and can supply it or work with one you've already purchased.

process.title: Permitted Installation
process.desc: We run the circuit, mount the charger, and schedule the inspection — all in one visit for most homes.

process.title: Test & Document
process.desc: We verify charge rate, hand over your rebate paperwork, and show you how to use the app if applicable.

faqs.q: Can my existing panel handle an EV charger?
faqs.a: Most 200A panels can accommodate a Level 2 charger without an upgrade. We'll confirm this in our free assessment before any work begins.

faqs.q: How long does the installation take?
faqs.a: Most installations are completed in 2–4 hours. Runs through finished walls or long conduit runs may take longer.
Yes. Many utilities and some states offer rebates of $200–$1,000 for home EV charger installation. We provide all documentation needed to claim them.

services.title: Outlet & Switch Installation
services.description: Add outlets, replace unsafe wiring, and upgrade switches throughout your home.
services.intro: Whether you need a new outlet in the kitchen, USB ports at the nightstand, or GFCI protection in the bathroom, we handle any outlet and switch project — cleanly, correctly, and to code.
features: GFCI outlet installation in kitchens, bathrooms, and garages
USB-A and USB-C combination outlets
20A dedicated circuits for appliances and equipment
Smart switch installation (Lutron, Leviton, Kasa, etc.)
3-way and 4-way switch replacement
Outdoor weatherproof outlet installation
Whole-home outlet inspection and replacement of worn devices
Clean drywall patching when new circuits are required

process.title: Needs Assessment
process.desc: We walk your space, identify what's needed, and explain options — including smart device compatibility.

process.title: Upfront Quote
process.desc: You get a flat-rate price before any work starts. No surprises, no hourly billing games.

process.title: Same-Day Service
process.desc: Most outlet and switch work is completed in a single visit with minimal disruption.

process.title: Test & Clean Up
process.desc: We test every device, clean up our work area, and walk you through anything new.

faqs.q: How many outlets can you add in a day?
faqs.a: We can typically add 4–8 outlets in a single visit depending on location and panel availability. We'll estimate time accurately before starting.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for outlet work?
faqs.a: Adding new circuits requires a permit in most jurisdictions. Replacing existing outlets typically does not. We handle permit requirements for every project.

faqs.q: What are GFCI outlets and where do I need them?
faqs.a: GFCI outlets interrupt power within milliseconds of detecting a ground fault. They're required by code in kitchens, bathrooms, garages, outdoors, and near water. Older homes often lack them.

services.title: Ceiling Fan & Lighting
services.description: Fan installation, recessed lighting, and fixture replacement done in one visit.
services.intro: Ceiling fans reduce energy costs year-round. Recessed lighting modernizes any room. We handle all fixture types — from basic swaps to full lighting design with dimmer controls and smart home integration.
features: Ceiling fan installation (with or without existing box)
Fan-rated junction box upgrade where required
Recessed LED lighting layout and installation
Dimmer switch installation and smart dimmer compatibility
Chandelier and pendant light installation
Under-cabinet LED lighting
Smart lighting integration (Lutron, Philips Hue, Kasa)
Outdoor security and landscape lighting circuits

process.title: Design Consultation
process.desc: We discuss your lighting goals, walk the rooms, and recommend fixture placement for the best effect.

process.title: Flat-Rate Quote
process.desc: You receive a written quote per fixture or per project — no hourly surprises.

process.title: Clean Installation
process.desc: We use drop cloths, install all fixtures correctly and level, and patch any ceiling work cleanly.

process.title: Full Test
process.desc: Every fixture and control is tested before we leave. We show you how to use any smart controls.

faqs.q: Can you install a ceiling fan where there's no existing fixture?
faqs.a: Yes. We can run a new circuit, install a fan-rated box, and have your fan up and running — all in one visit.

faqs.q: What's the difference between a regular and fan-rated electrical box?
faqs.a: Fan-rated boxes are braced to handle the dynamic load of a spinning fan. Installing a fan on a standard light box is a safety hazard — we always upgrade if needed.

faqs.q: How many recessed lights do I need per room?
faqs.a: A common rule is one fixture per 4–6 square feet of ceiling area for general lighting. We'll lay it out for optimal coverage and ambiance.

services.title: Whole-Home Rewiring
services.description: Complete rewiring of older homes to eliminate knob-and-tube, aluminum wiring, and fire hazards.
services.intro: Homes built before 1970 often contain knob-and-tube or aluminum wiring that modern appliances and insurance companies won't tolerate. A full rewire gives you safe, grounded circuits throughout — and may be required before your insurer will renew your policy.
features: Complete replacement of knob-and-tube or aluminum wiring
New grounded circuits throughout the home
Panel upgrade included where needed
Smoke and CO detector installation to current code
Arc-fault protection on all bedroom and living area circuits
Staged work to minimize disruption to occupied homes
All permits pulled and inspections scheduled
Insurance documentation letter provided after completion

process.title: Full Home Evaluation
process.desc: We assess all accessible wiring, note hazard locations, and build a complete scope before pricing.

process.title: Phased Plan
process.desc: We sequence the work to keep your home livable — typically room by room over several days.

process.title: Permitted Rewire
process.desc: All new wiring is run to NEC standards with inspections at rough-in and completion.

process.title: Final Documentation
process.desc: You receive a completion letter for your insurance company and a copy of all permits and inspection records.

faqs.q: How long does a whole-home rewire take?
faqs.a: Most single-family homes take 3–7 days depending on size and accessibility. We'll give you an accurate timeline in your estimate.

faqs.q: Will I need to move out during the rewire?
faqs.a: Not usually. We stage the work so you always have power to key areas. We'll discuss the schedule and any temporary disruptions upfront.

faqs.q: My insurance company is threatening to cancel my policy over old wiring. Can you help?
faqs.a: Yes. We provide a completion letter that most insurance carriers accept. We've helped many homeowners retain coverage after a rewire.

services.title: Generator Installation
services.description: Standby and portable generator hookups with transfer switches — never lose power again.
services.intro: A whole-home standby generator turns on automatically within seconds of a power outage. We handle full installation — pad, gas line coordination, transfer switch, and permit — so you're protected before the next storm hits.
features: Generac, Kohler, or Briggs & Stratton generator installation
Automatic transfer switch (ATS) installed to code
Dedicated circuit panel for critical loads
Concrete pad or composite pad installation
Natural gas or LP fuel line coordination
Load testing and system commissioning
Manual transfer switch for portable generator hookups
Permit and utility notification handled

process.title: Load Assessment
process.desc: We calculate your critical loads to right-size the generator — too small fails when you need it, too large wastes money.

process.title: Site Planning
process.desc: We select the optimal generator location meeting code clearances from windows, doors, and gas meters.

process.title: Full Installation
process.desc: Pad, transfer switch, wiring, and gas connection are all completed in a single 1–2 day installation.

process.title: Test & Training
process.desc: We run a full load test, register your warranty, and walk you through startup and maintenance procedures.

faqs.q: What size generator do I need?
faqs.a: A 10–14kW generator covers most homes' critical loads (HVAC, fridge, lights, outlets). A 20–22kW unit powers nearly everything. We do a load calculation to match your needs.

faqs.q: Can I connect my portable generator to the house?
faqs.a: Yes, with a properly installed manual transfer switch. Never backfeed a generator directly into an outlet — it's illegal and can electrocute utility workers.

faqs.q: How often does a standby generator need maintenance?
faqs.a: Generac and Kohler recommend annual service — oil change, spark plugs, air filter, and battery test. Many customers sign up for our annual maintenance plan.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from problem to powered up.

steps.title: Free Estimate
steps.desc: A licensed electrician assesses your project and gives you a written flat-rate quote — no hourly surprises.

steps.title: Permit Pulled
steps.desc: We handle all permit applications so your work is legal, inspected, and fully documented.

steps.title: Clean Installation
steps.desc: Our team works efficiently, protects your home, and completes most jobs in a single visit.

steps.title: Inspected & Guaranteed
steps.desc: We meet the inspector, pass the inspection, and back every job with our workmanship warranty.

serviceOptions: Not Sure — I Need an Assessment

items.q: How do I know if my electrical panel needs to be replaced?
items.a: Signs include frequently tripping breakers, a panel older than 30 years, Federal Pacific or Zinsco brand (recalled for fire risk), burning smell, or inability to add circuits for new appliances or an EV charger.
Yes. We hold a Master Electrician license and carry full general liability and workers' compensation insurance. Certificates of insurance are available on request.

items.q: Do you pull permits for every job?
items.a: We pull permits on all work that legally requires one — panel upgrades, new circuits, generator installs, and rewires. This protects your insurance coverage and your home's resale value.

items.q: How soon can you come out?
items.a: We offer same-day and next-day service for most jobs. Emergency electrical service is available 24/7 for safety hazards.
Yes. We provide free written estimates for all projects before any work begins. Our quotes are flat-rate — the price you're quoted is the price you pay.

cta.subtext: Get a free estimate from a licensed master electrician — same-day availability, flat-rate pricing, and work that's inspected and guaranteed.
